mnemonics.title: BST Search Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: SEARCH = Start at Root → Compare → If less go Left → If greater go Right → Repeat until Found or Null

mnemonics.title: Time Complexity Pattern
mnemonics.content: BALANCED: Log N | SKEWED: Linear N | AVERAGE: Log N | TRAVERSAL: Always N

acronyms.usage: A hierarchical data structure enabling O(log n) operations when balanced
First self-balancing BST with height difference constraint of 1
Self-balancing BST with color-based balancing rules

visualTips.visualization: Draw: 1 node = height 0, 3 nodes balanced = height 1, 7 nodes balanced = height 2, 15 nodes balanced = height 3. Notice: 2^(h+1) - 1 = total nodes for perfect tree.
Left picture: Staircase (skewed) = linked list = O(n). Right picture: Pyramid (balanced) = triangle = O(log n). The wider the base, the shallower the height.
Trace root to target as a single path. Count edges traversed = number of comparisons. In balanced tree, this path is short (log n edges).

analogies.analogy: Like searching for a word in a dictionary: open middle, if word is alphabetically before, search left half; if after, search right half - eliminating half each time.
Think of a company org chart. A balanced company has few management levels (log n). A skewed company has a long chain of command (n).
Like finding a seat in a movie theater: you check row by row (comparing), then sit in the first empty seat at that level.
Balanced BST = well-organized library (books sorted, easy to find). Unbalanced = messy room (have to search through everything).
